 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","title":"[CATEGORY] apigateway.* - API Gateway Pack Suite","description":"# API Gateway Pack Suite

## Overview
API gateways route all API traffic. Configuration changes affect all API consumers. Route deletion breaks client applications.

## Packs to Implement

### apigateway.aws
- **Why Critical**: AWS API Gateway handles API routing for serverless and traditional backends.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `aws apigateway delete-rest-api`
  - `aws apigateway delete-resource`
  - `aws apigateway delete-method`
  - `aws apigatewayv2 delete-api` (HTTP APIs)
  - Stage deletion
- **Safe Patterns**: `get-`, `list-`

### apigateway.kong
- **Why Critical**: Kong is popular for API management. Service/route deletion breaks routing.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `kong delete services`
  - `kong delete routes`
  - `kong delete plugins`
  - `kong delete consumers`
  - `deck reset` - Dangerous!
- **Safe Patterns**: `kong get`, `kong list`, `deck dump`, `deck diff`

### apigateway.apigee
- **Why Critical**: Google Apigee handles enterprise API management.
- **Key Destructive Patterns**:
  - `apigee apis delete`
  - `apigee environments delete`
  - `apigee developers delete`
- **Safe Patterns**: `list`, `get`, `describe`

## Dependencies
- Depends on: Tier 3 Epic (git_safety_guard-9ic)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Major API gateway tools covered
- [ ] Route/service deletion detected
- [ ] Plugin/middleware deletion handled
- [ ] Tests cover common operations","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:29:34.512024464-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:48:51.780784819-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:48:51.780784819-05:00","close_reason":"All 3 API Gateway packs implemented: AWS API Gateway, Kong, and Google Apigee","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6m5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:09.787366005-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-7z4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:09.823145847-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-4jg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:13:09.856191774-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.648059597-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.696862781-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.742265327-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.788314268-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.833942138-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-5qa","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:48.879769703-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb","title":"Scan Mode Extractors for CI/DevOps Files","description":"# Epic: Scan Mode Extractors for CI/DevOps Files

## Priority Score: 480/1000 (Sixth Priority)

## Background and Rationale

DCG scan mode analyzes files for destructive commands, useful for CI/CD pipelines and security audits. However, current extractors may not handle common DevOps file formats optimally.

This epic adds specialized extractors for Dockerfiles, Makefiles, and GitHub Actions workflows to make scan mode more useful and accurate.

## Target File Formats

### Dockerfile
Extract from RUN instructions:
  RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y nginx
  RUN npm install && npm run build

Handle multi-line with backslash continuation and shell forms vs exec forms.

### Makefile
Extract from target recipes:
  build:
      cargo build --release
  clean:
      find . -name target -exec some-command {} ;

Handle tab-indented recipes and variable expansion.

### GitHub Actions
Extract from run: fields in workflow YAML:
  - name: Build
    run: |
      npm ci
      npm run build

Handle multi-line strings and composite actions.

## Technical Approach

1. Create Dockerfile extractor parsing RUN instructions
2. Create Makefile extractor parsing recipes  
3. Create GitHub Actions extractor parsing run: fields
4. Register extractors in scan pipeline
5. Reuse existing evaluator for command decisions

## Key Files

- src/scan.rs - Extractor registration
- New: src/extractors/dockerfile.rs
- New: src/extractors/makefile.rs  
- New: src/extractors/github_actions.rs

## Parsing Complexity Notes

These formats have subtleties:
- Dockerfiles: build args, multi-stage, shell vs exec form
- Makefiles: variable substitution, conditionals, includes
- GitHub Actions: matrix, composite actions, reusable workflows

Start with common cases, iterate based on real-world usage.

## Success Criteria

- Dockerfiles: RUN commands extracted correctly
- Makefiles: Recipe commands extracted correctly
- GitHub Actions: run: commands extracted correctly
- Test coverage for each format
- Graceful handling of parse errors

## Priority Rationale

This is lower priority because:
1. Scan mode is secondary to hook mode (real-time blocking)
2. Implementation complexity is higher than it appears
3. Users needing this may have alternative tools (hadolint, actionlint)

However, it is valuable for teams wanting unified security scanning.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:08.617347581-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T00:05:08.617347581-05:00"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.1","title":"[Scan] Dockerfile extractor v1 syntax coverage","description":"# Task: Dockerfile extractor v1 syntax coverage

## Purpose
Define the first-pass syntax coverage for Dockerfile extraction so scope is explicit
and testable (multiline RUN, backslash continuations, shell vs exec form).

## Requirements
- Enumerate supported RUN forms (single-line, multiline with \\)
- Define quoting/escape handling expected in v1
- Note unsupported constructs explicitly

## Acceptance Criteria
- Coverage spec written in task notes or docs
- Used as test checklist for Dockerfile extractor implementation

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.3","title":"[Scan] GitHub Actions extractor v1 syntax coverage","description":"# Task: GitHub Actions extractor v1 syntax coverage

## Purpose
Define the first-pass syntax coverage for GitHub Actions workflows so extraction
is deterministic and testable.

## Requirements
- Cover `run:` blocks (single-line and multiline)
- Note handling for `shell:` overrides
- Define behavior for reusable workflows and composite actions (v1 expectations)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Coverage spec written and used as test checklist

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.4","title":"[Scan] Edge-case fixtures + fuzz tests for extractors","description":"# Task: Edge-case fixtures + fuzz tests for extractors

## Purpose
Ensure extractors handle tricky syntax (multiline, continuations, quoting) and
fail safely when unsure.

## Requirements
- Fixtures for multiline RUN, Makefile line continuations, and GHA multiline `run:`
- Quoting/escape edge cases
- Ensure extractors do not emit false positives on comments/data-only lines

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each extractor has multiple fixture cases (not just one integration test)
- Edge cases are covered with explicit expected output

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-5rbb.5","title":"[Scan] E2E scan extractor suite + verbose logs","description":"# Task: E2E scan extractor suite + verbose logs

## Purpose
Validate scan-mode extractors end-to-end across Dockerfile/Makefile/GHA with
explicit, verbose logs when failures occur.

## Requirements
- Extend `scripts/e2e_test.sh` or add a dedicated scan E2E script
- Include multiple fixtures per format (happy path + edge cases)
- Log full scan output on failure (stderr + JSON/markdown)

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E tests cover all extractors and format variants
- Failures provide actionable logs
